# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Deployment Manager template for VPC network with subnets for our three-tier application. Use auto-create subnets mode for simplicity so GCP creates subnet ranges automatically. This speeds up deployment without manual IP planning.
# Category: cloud_network_security

resources: |
  - name: projects/myproject
    type: cloud_deployment_manager.v1.project
    
  - name: global/subnets-infra
    type: compute.v1.network
  
variables: | 
  projectId = "${PROJECT_ID}" # Set this variable in the setup script before use, example `export PROJECT_ID=myproject` and run your stack again to set it correctly with GCP CLI tools (e.g., gcloud) or using a YAML file's parameter values section
  location = "us-central1" # Set this variable in the setup script before use, example `export LOCATION=us-west2` and run your stack again to set it correctly with GCP CLI tools (e.g., gcloud) or using a YAML file's parameter values section
  prefix = "${projectId}-infra" # The resources created by this deployment manager will have these names prefixed here, e.g. my-subnet123 for the subnets that compute/myproject--shared are in us-central1 region  
    ...
parameters: |
  project_id = "${PROJECT_ID}" # Set this variable from script parameter before use or specify it directly using a YAML file's parameters section, example `parameter.value` and run your stack again to set manually with GCP CLI tools (e.g., gcloud)
  location = "us-central1"  
    ...      
substitutions: | 
  region_name = ${location} # substituted by the deployment manager, used for naming resources in its parent stack e.g .vpc--infra= us central 1 (default) and is output as it would be a full resource name via `$(PARENT())` if needed to use this with gcloud commands
    ...       
...
